title: When Beauty Is Bald
content:
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve sung of Honorï؟½s golden hair
آ آ آ آ And Heroï؟½s auburn tresses,
آ آ آ آ Of Bellaï؟½s back abundance, where
آ آ آ آ The sun throws his caresses;
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve sung of curl, and coil, and braid;
آ آ آ آ On meshes Iï؟½ve dilated,
آ آ آ آ Until at last Iï؟½m sore afraid
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s nothing re the hair of maid
آ آ آ آ That I have left unstated.
آ آ آ آ 
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Twill much relieve the constant strain
آ آ آ آ Of rhyming to extol her
آ آ آ آ When on the roof of Sophieï؟½s brain
آ آ آ آ Appears a bright cupola.
آ آ آ آ The poetï؟½s verse will freshly run,
آ آ آ آ Effects will come much faster,
آ آ آ آ If he may tell the darling one
آ آ آ آ Her skull is glowing like the sun
آ آ آ آ And smooth as alabaster.
آ آ آ آ 
آ آ آ آ New stimulus the singer nerves,
آ آ آ آ When beauty, scorning switches,
آ آ آ آ Adds to her many swelling curves
آ آ آ آ A baldness that bewitches.
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ve sung too many wigs, I swear,
آ آ آ آ And now the poet mocks myths,
آ آ آ آ For Juliet in her head of air
آ آ آ آ Outshines the moon, and everywhere,
آ آ آ آ Love really laughs at locksmiths.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Dyson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Edward Dyson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
